subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the psychological and emotional development of Kezia and her father
Understand the deeper themes, motifs, and moral lessons of the story
Critically evaluate the role of authority, fear, and affection in child development
Identify literary devices such as imagery, foreshadowing, and characterization
Develop advanced reading comprehension, inference, and critical thinking skills
Apply story insights to real-life family and social contexts

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Contextual Analysis
topics_covered: Historical and cultural background of the story
Exploring the family dynamics and societal expectations
teaching_strategy: Board diagrams showing family hierarchy and relationships
student_activities: Discuss how authority figures affect children’s emotions
Predict story conflicts based on title and introduction

lesson_plan.title: Close Reading – Character Study
topics_covered: Father’s character traits: strictness, concern, and transformation
teaching_strategy: Guided discussion on inferred character motivations
student_activities: Extract textual evidence for character analysis
Explain why characters respond as they do

lesson_plan.title: Analyzing Key Events and Psychological Impact
topics_covered: Speech practice and its emotional effect on Kezia
Father’s realization and change in behavior
teaching_strategy: Diagram mapping of events and emotional responses
student_activities: Write 3–4 sentences on emotional changes in characters

lesson_plan.title: Themes, Literary Devices, and Moral Analysis
topics_covered: Main theme: Understanding, empathy, and family bonds
Use of literary devices: imagery, foreshadowing, and characterization
Moral lessons and psychological insights from the story
teaching_strategy: Group discussion of devices and their effect
student_activities: Discuss in groups how they enhance the story

lesson_plan.title: Critical Summary and Inference
topics_covered: Sequence of events and character growth
Connecting story events to real-life family and social scenarios
teaching_strategy: Flowchart of events and relationships on board
Guided critical discussion for making inferences
student_activities: Create a detailed timeline of events
Write an 8–10 sentence critical summary including inferences

lesson_plan.title: Vocabulary, Reflection, and Real-life Application
topics_covered: Reflective writing on character emotions and relationships
Applying story insights to personal and family life
teaching_strategy: Board explanation of advanced vocabulary and example sentences
Reflective writing prompts for personal connection
student_activities: List new words with meanings and use in sentences
Write a reflective paragraph on lessons learned
Discuss how understanding emotions can improve relationships

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze emotional development, infer motivations, identify literary devices, and relate story insights to real-life family and social situations.
